rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JUDITH SPEEDLING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgement, Pursuant To Probate Code Section 2580(B)(8), For Authority To Liquidate Ymca 401(A) Retirement Fund Account
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petition does not allege the conservatee's position. The court cannot determine if the conservatee is or is not opposed to this proposed action. PC 2582(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Deadline is 6/29/23 for delivering the proposed order and endorsed filed courtesy copy of the proof of service of the correct/complete Notice of Hearing to the assigned examiner. Appearance will be required on 7/11/23 if these issues are not addressed timely.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL L BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The ex parte petitions were never adjudicated because issues raised by the reviewing examiners were never addressed in pleadings FILED for the record. Additional attorney fees for litigation are being allowed solely because the other heirs consented. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HEIDEMARIE WILMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/28/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) No proof of service has been filed to show a copy of the supplement (filed on 04/03/2023) was served on each person who requested special notice. See Probate Code section 1252(a) & CRC 7.53(b). This issue was included in the tentative ruling for the 04/25/2023 hearing, but has not been addressed. 2) No statement of the Chase Bank accounts has been filed to verify the ending cash balance of each account stated on Schedules E & F. The statement must cover a period including 06/08/2021. 3) Schedules E & F show "cash in transit" and proceeds from the annuities, but the distribution checks were issued in April 2021, before the ending date of this account. Did the petitioner not cash those checks before 06/08/2021? Moreover, the proceed amounts stated on Schedules E & F do not match the distribution check amounts. 4) A copy of the response to the objections filed on 05/30/2023 must be served on the former attorney for the objector who requested special notice.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TUAN VAN TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/25/23 for failure to address Examiner's Notes. NB: the amended notice of hearing filed on 5/3/23 contained the wrong hearing date and did not provide direct notice to the guardian ad litem. All required documents to be filed and provided by 7/10/23.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE PATRICIA PRONGOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Furthermore, although file stamped on 5/23/23, the amended petition was not actually e-filed until 5/24/23, when it was first available for viewing on the Court's website. Notice of the 7/26/23 hearing of the amended petition must be given at least 15 days before 7/26/23. 2. Original bank statements still have not been filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. 3. The decedent died on 6/16/20 and had over $1,000,000 in a bank account, and the co-administrators were granted Letters on 2/1/21, but no interest was earned 4/10/22? 4. There were two payments to the FTB on 1/9/23 and 3/14/23 for just "State Tax." Please explain. Endorsed filed courtesy copies any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/14/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM M. COERTNIK REVOCABLE TRUST DATED JULY 22, 1992
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets (Heggstad Petition)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. An IRA may be established and held by an individual. The Internal Revenue Code defines an IRA as a trust created or organized in the United States for the exclusive benefit of an individual or his beneficiaries. 26 U.S.C. sections 408(a) & 408A(a). Other provisions of that section also appear to require that the party establishing and holding the IRA to be an individual. See section 408(a)(4), (d)(3)(C)(ii)(I), (d)(6), (e)(2)(A), (e)(4). The regulations promulgated in connection with section 408 contain similar descriptions of the IRA holder as an individual. See 26 C.F.R. section 1.408-2(b), (b)(4), (b)(6), and (b)(8). Thus, a legal entity, such as a revocable living trust, since it is not an individual, does not appear to qualify as the holder of an IRA. Further, a transfer of an IRA to a revocable living trust would be a prohibited transaction under section 408(e)(2)(A), in that an IRA loses its tax-exempt status and ceases to be an IRA if, during any taxable year of the individual for whose benefit any IRA is established, that individual engages in any transaction prohibited by 26 U.S.C. section 4975. The definition of prohibited transaction includes a transfer to, or use by or for the benefit of, a disqualified person of the income or assets of a plan (section 4975(c)(1)(D)). The definition of a disqualified person includes both a fiduciary (section 4975(e)(2)(A)) and trust or estate, 50 percent or more of which is owned by a fiduciary (section 4975(e)(2)(G)). Moreover, a transfer of an IRA to a revocable trust would potentially violate the Internal Revenue Code's prohibition against the commingling of IRA assets with other property. See section 408(a)(5); see also regulations section 1.408-2(b)(5)(i). If the transfer of the Roth IRA would have constituted a prohibited transaction and would have violated commingling prohibitions, did the settlor truly intend to transfer the Roth IRA to the trust by executing the general assignment of personal property? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DEA FAMILY 1993 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish Trust Claim Of Ownership Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/11/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/18/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) Gilbert Dea as successor trustee of the May Fong Trust must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(1). 4) The first paragraph of Article Fifth, Section B, of the trust appears to require the surviving trustor to appoint a successor trustee. 5) Schedule B listing the Fortuna property was not signed by the deceased trustor. Moreover, Schedule B does not appear to have been identified or referred to in the trust, unlike Schedule A specifically referred to in the second introductory paragraph and Article Second of the trust. 6) Did the deceased trustor sign a general assignment or any other document purporting to transfer his interest in the Fortuna property to the trust?

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/28/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 05/09/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to provide the petitioner's telephone number in addition to his address (at item 2 of acknowledgment of receipt). 4) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. 5) Item 3g(d) of the petition must be completed re James Riley as first-named executor. 6) Re item 8: i) The date of death must be provided for the decedent's spouse and two children. ii) The date of death of Moses Riley must be provided. iii) Did Elaine Hubbard leave any issue who survived her? 7) The decedent died more than 6 years ago.
